reasoning: France and Spain sit neck-and-neck atop the outright markets, but Spain's flat 0-0 draw with Cape Verde nudged France to solo favorite while La Roja absorbs injuries to Fermin Lopez and Samu plus fitness management of Yamal and Williams. I lean France for their two-final pedigree, fit Mbappe and elite midfield depth despite losing Ekitike and Kamara, though their group with Senegal and Norway is among the tougher top-seed paths. The chasing pack of Portugal, England, Brazil and defending champion Argentina is tightly bunched, so confidence is genuinely low this early. Morocco is the dark horse: a 2022 semifinalist with a deep, experienced squad whose only blemish so far is a 1-1 draw with Brazil, though they are managing fitness concerns. Main threats to France are an in-form Germany (7-1 opener) and the host nations riding crowd and altitude/travel advantages in the knockout rounds.
